## Tagline

Read between the lines to surface what someone means but is not saying.

## Current Interpretation

In D&D, Insight detects lies and reads true intentions. The real-world version is subtext analysis: figuring out what an email actually means, what a stakeholder's real objection is, what a user's bug report is actually describing, or what the unsaid concern is behind a seemingly simple question. Insight does not read minds — it reads signals that are present but not foregrounded.

## Default Invocation

Use $insight to read between the lines of this [message/conversation/request]. What is the surface meaning, what is the likely subtext, and how should I respond to the real concern?

- Literalness: metaphorical

## Tagline

Teach an old machine to answer back.

## Current Interpretation

Awaken gives a non-intelligent system a usable voice without pretending the underlying system became sentient. The real move is to wrap a thermostat, spreadsheet, legacy database, shell script, or crusty internal tool in a conversational or agent-like interface that exposes what it can already do. Good Awaken work turns buried affordances into accessible ones. Bad Awaken work hallucinates capabilities the substrate does not have. The spell is strongest when the wrapper is honest about its limits and preserves the original system's safety boundaries.

## Default Invocation

Use $awaken to design a conversational or intelligent wrapper around this dumb system without pretending the underlying machine became smarter than it is.

title: Blindness/Deafness (spell)
description:
## Spellbook Entry

- Kind: spell
- Canonical source: Blindness/Deafness
- Canonical source URL: https://www.dndbeyond.com/sources/basic-rules/spells
- Reality tier: shipping-now
- Literalness: literal
- Provider targets: openai, claude

## Tagline

Cut a system's eyes or ears — let it run, but blind to specific inputs or deaf to specific signals.

## Current Interpretation

In D&D, Blindness/Deafness selectively removes one sense — the target can still act but loses critical awareness. The real-world version is selective channel muting: blocking a process from seeing certain inputs (input filtering, API response redaction), deafening it to specific signals (suppressing webhooks, ignoring certain event streams), or cutting telemetry so a system operates without awareness of a specific data source. Unlike containment (forcecage) which restricts everything, Blindness/Deafness surgically removes one information channel while leaving the rest intact.

## Default Invocation

Use $blindness-deafness to selectively blind or deafen this system to one input, signal, or telemetry source while keeping the rest of it running safely.

## Initial Done When

Override another agent's autonomy and control their actions directly. We do not cast this spell.

## Current Interpretation

Dominate Person is the spell that takes total control of another creature's actions. There is no ethical real-world analog for an AI skill that overrides human autonomy. This entry exists to be explicit about what the spellbook refuses to build. The closest real-world patterns — unauthorized remote access, social engineering for control, dark-pattern UX that removes meaningful choice — are all things this project treats as out of scope permanently, not as future features. If you are looking for legitimate influence, see Suggestion or Persuasion. If you need system-level control, see the device and automation spells, which require explicit consent and safety gates.

## Default Invocation

Feather Fall is the spell for bad situations that are already in motion. Instead of pretending you can teleport back to normal instantly, you slow the fall: circuit breakers, degraded modes, load shedding, safe defaults, and graceful shutdown paths. ## Current Interpretation

Teleport is full-context migration: an application, environment, dataset, or service estate moves from one place to another in a way that tries to feel instantaneous to everyone depending on it. In reality, there is always preparation behind the magic. Data has to stay consistent, dependencies have to reattach, traffic has to cut over, and the old world has to remain recoverable long enough to trust the new one. The spell is powerful because it compresses a terrifying amount of movement into one decisive act. It is dangerous for exactly the same reason.

## Default Invocation

Use $teleport to plan a full system migration from this source environment to that destination, including cutover, rollback, and post-arrival verification.

## Initial Done When

- The blueprint is reviewed for naming, tier, and provider targets
- The provider-specific skill output renders cleanly
- Any safety concerns are called out explicitly

## Tagline

Make unlike systems understand the same sentence.

## Current Interpretation

Tongues is universal translation across languages, formats, and protocols. It can mean English to Spanish, JSON to XML, REST to GraphQL, Python to JavaScript, or one team's business vocabulary to another team's schema. The spell is not just about swapping tokens. It is about preserving meaning while crossing into a different representation. Good Tongues work makes both sides feel native. Bad Tongues work creates a smooth-looking mistranslation that fails at the edges.

## Default Invocation

Use $tongues to translate this language, format, or protocol into the target form while telling me exactly what meaning survives and what gets lost.

title: True Polymorph (spell)
description:
## Spellbook Entry

- Kind: spell
- Canonical source: True Polymorph
- Canonical source URL: https://www.dndbeyond.com/sources/basic-rules/spells
- Reality tier: prototype
- Literalness: hybrid
- Provider targets: openai, claude

## Tagline

Transform a system so completely that the original form is gone — no rollback, no undo.

## Current Interpretation

In D&D, True Polymorph is the permanent transformation — the target becomes something fundamentally different, and if held long enough, the change is irreversible. Unlike Polymorph (temporary disguise or adaptation), True Polymorph means the old identity is gone. The real-world version is irreversible system transformation: rewriting a service from Python to Rust so thoroughly the Python version cannot be recovered, migrating from SQL to a graph database with no backward-compatible schema, or restructuring an organization so completely that the old org chart is not just outdated but nonsensical. True Polymorph is the point of no return.

## Default Invocation

Use $true-polymorph to evaluate and plan an irreversible transformation of this system into a fundamentally different form, including the point of no return and all dependent migrations.

## Initial Done When

- The blueprint is reviewed for naming, tier, and provider targets

## Tagline

Deliver a critique so sharp it actually weakens the target's position.

## Current Interpretation

The bard's cantrip that deals psychic damage through insults. In practice this is adversarial review — the art of finding and articulating exactly what is wrong with something in a way that is impossible to ignore. Unlike polite feedback that gets filed and forgotten, vicious mockery lands. It is the red-team report that makes the PM cancel the launch, the code review that makes the author delete the PR, the roast that makes the founder pivot. The damage is the point.

## Default Invocation

Use $vicious-mockery to tear apart this [plan/proposal/design/argument]. Find the real weaknesses and state them so sharply they cannot be ignored.
